domain: F degrees of freedom above 1E5 (measured accuracy envelope; the both-large orientation binds, crossing near df 1.5E5-3E5)
cause: incomplete-beta continued fraction loses accuracy while its local convergence test still passes
observed_effect: without the guard, errors grow to ~4E-7 by df~1E9; the public F surface now REJECTS df > 1E5 with CVErr(xlErrNum)
notes: Strict validated-domain policy (PROB_F_ValidateEnvelope). F_Density is closed-form and unrestricted. Distinct from the LogBeta normalization defect.

domain: upper tail beyond the central region (standardized z above ~2.75-3.25)
cause: the standard-normal survival core loses RELATIVE accuracy in the deep tail (absolute error stays ~1E-17)
observed_effect: relative error grows monotonically: ~1E-14 at z=3, ~1E-13 at z=4, ~5E-11 at z=5, ~5E-10 at z=6
notes: Tight central contracts are domain-restricted accordingly. Still far better than 1-CDF cancellation; only the RELATIVE bound degrades, not the absolute.

domain: both shapes sufficiently small (roughly a, b below 1E-3), interior probabilities
cause: For sufficiently small positive Beta shapes the distribution becomes numerically indistinguishable in binary64 from a two-point limiting distribution with an interior CDF plateau approximately equal to b/(a+b). For some probabilities the mathematical quantile lies below the smallest positive Double or closer to one than the nearest representable interior Double.
observed_effect: No representable interior quantile or meaningful forward round-trip exists; e.g. at a=1E-12, b=9.9E-14 the interior CDF is flat at ~0.0901 across the whole representable interval and the p=0.5 quantile needs 1-x ~ 1E-1E13.
evidence: benchmark tiny_unbalanced_representable regime; points outside it have no representable quantile
notes: Floating-point representability boundary, NOT inverse-solver non-convergence. Value-accuracy contracts are scoped to the representable region; the collapsed region carries behavioral tests only.

domain: both degrees of freedom sufficiently small, interior probabilities
cause: F_CDF(x; d1, d2) = I_y(d1/2, d2/2), so small d1 and d2 inherit the Beta two-point collapse described in BetaInverse.InteriorQuantileRepresentability.
observed_effect: The interior CDF plateaus and the mathematical quantile leaves the representable Double range for probabilities away from the plateau.
notes: Same representability boundary as the Beta inverse, reached through the half-degree-of-freedom shapes.
